items.title: Developer changelog
items.author: developers.openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active with API updates heading into April 2026. Major model releases include GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano added to the Chat Completions and Responses API, with GPT-5.4 mini targeting high-volume workloads and nano optimized for speed and cost. GPT-5.3-Codex was also launched as an agentic coding model combining the Codex and GPT-5 training stacks, running ~25% faster than predecessors with new benchmark highs. On the research side, GPT-Rosalind was introduced as a life sciences

items.title: Introducing GPT-5.3 Instant, GPT-5.4 Thinking, and GPT-5.4 Pro — OpenAI Academy
items.author: academy.openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active with major announcements in April 2026. On April 16, 2026, the company introduced GPT-Rosalind, a frontier reasoning model purpose-built for life sciences research — targeting biology, drug discovery, protein engineering, genomics, and translational medicine. The same day, OpenAI announced the next evolution of its Agents SDK, expanding autonomous agent capabilities for developers. items.title: The Walt Disney Company and OpenAI reach landmark agreement for Sora
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active on Twitter (@OpenAI) in April 2026 with several major announcements. The company announced updates to ChatGPT Pro and Plus subscriptions to better support the growing use of Codex, introducing a new $100/month Pro tier offering 5x more Codex usage than Plus, optimized for longer, high-effort coding sessions. items.title: Summary of the latest GPT-Image-2 intelligence: 5 major capability upgrades and release date prediction (April 2026)
items.author: help.apiyi.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been on an aggressive release cadence in early 2026. GPT-5.4 launched in March 2026, bringing together advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows. It incorporates the coding capabilities of GPT-5.3-Codex, reports a 33% reduction in factual errors vs GPT-5.2, and includes built-in computer use and improved deep research. The GPT-5.4 Thinking variant adds an upfront reasoning plan so users can adjust the model's direction mid-response. Smaller variants — GPT-5.4 mini and nano

items.title: GPT as a Measurement Tool (OpenAI PDF)
items.author: cdn.openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI's recent research output in early 2026 spans several high-impact areas. Notable publications include work on chain-of-thought monitorability (evaluating misalignment risks in coding agents), an instruction hierarchy challenge paper (IH-Challenge) that trains models to resist prompt injection and prioritize trusted instructions, and a FrontierScience benchmark evaluating AI's ability to autonomously perform scientific research tasks. OpenAI also published 'AI as a Scientific Collaborator'

items.title: New AI Model Releases News | April 2026 (Startup Edition)
items.author: blog.mean.ceo
items.snippet: OpenAI has been on an aggressive release cadence in early 2026. GPT-5.4 launched in March 2026, bringing together advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows. It incorporates the coding capabilities of GPT-5.3-Codex, reports a 33% reduction in factual errors vs GPT-5.2, and includes built-in computer use and improved deep research. The GPT-5.4 Thinking variant adds an upfront reasoning plan so users can adjust the model's direction mid-response. Smaller variants — GPT-5.4 mini and nano
